feedback.text: The video game industry has been implementing their games to require an internet connection to their servers in order to even start up, regardless of whether the game offers single-player, multiplayer or both types of game modes. Although any software support will end at some point, games that feature single-player modes should still remain functional, allowing players (copy owners) to access those specific modes even after official support ends and any multiplayer servers are shut down. By definition a single-player game mode does not require participation of other players and as such there is no valid technical or business justification for it to require a connection to a server. As a paying customer, I would like a guarantee through legal regulation that any video game product I bought will remain in a reasonably functional state and single player game modes will remain playable even after the official support ends.
